Chinese · EP2
Soy Braised Pork Belly 🐷
Rich, sticky & deeply savoury
Cook time60 min
DifficultyMedium
Serves2–3
Ingredients
  • 500g pork belly, cut into chunks
  • 3 tbsp dark soy sauce
  • 2 tbsp light soy sauce
  • 2 tbsp Shaoxing rice wine
  • 1 tbsp sugar (rock or brown)
  • 3 cloves garlic, smashed
  • 2 slices fresh ginger
  • 2 soy-marinated eggs
  • Bok choy & sesame seeds to serve
Method
  1. 1Blanch pork belly in boiling water for 5 mins. Drain and pat dry.
  2. 2Sear pork in a hot pan until golden on all sides.
  3. 3Add soy sauces, rice wine, sugar, garlic, ginger and enough water to just cover. Bring to boil.
  4. 4Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 40–45 mins until tender and sauce has thickened.
  5. 5Blanch bok choy. Serve pork over rice, drizzle sauce over, add soy egg and garnish.
💡 Highlight Tips
Blanching removes impurities — don't skip it for a cleaner sauce.
Use rock sugar instead of brown sugar for a glossier, restaurant-style glaze.
Make ahead — tastes even better the next day once the flavours deepen.
Thai · EP2
Thai Basil Pork Rice 🌶️
Spicy, aromatic & 15 mins flat
Cook time15 min
DifficultyEasy
Serves1–2
Ingredients
  • 300g minced pork
  • Large handful holy basil (or Thai basil)
  • 4 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2–3 bird's eye chillies, sliced
  • 2 tbsp oyster sauce
  • 1 tbsp fish sauce
  • 1 tsp dark soy sauce
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • 1–2 eggs + jasmine rice to serve
Method
  1. 1Heat oil in a wok on high heat. Fry garlic and chilli for 30 seconds until fragrant.
  2. 2Add mince. Break up and stir-fry until cooked through, about 4–5 mins.
  3. 3Add oyster sauce, fish sauce, soy and sugar. Toss to coat.
  4. 4Turn off heat, stir in basil leaves — residual heat will wilt them.
  5. 5Fry egg in hot oil until crispy edges form. Serve on rice with the pork.
💡 Highlight Tips
Use a screaming hot wok — that's the secret to the wok hei (smoky flavour).
Add basil OFF the heat so it stays bright and fragrant, not muddy.
Crispy fried egg is non-negotiable — get the oil very hot before cracking it in.
Fusion · EP2
Creamy Chicken & Sweetcorn 🌽
Comforting, silky & crowd-pleasing
Cook time25 min
DifficultyEasy
Serves2
Ingredients
  • 2 chicken breasts, sliced thin
  • 1 tin sweetcorn (or 2 ears fresh)
  • 150ml double cream
  • 150ml chicken stock
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • ½ tsp smoked paprika
  • Salt, pepper & fresh parsley
  • Jasmine rice to serve
Method
  1. 1Season chicken with salt, pepper and paprika. Pan-fry in butter until golden, set aside.
  2. 2In the same pan, fry garlic for 1 min. Add sweetcorn and stir for 2 mins.
  3. 3Pour in stock and cream. Simmer on medium for 5–6 mins until sauce thickens.
  4. 4Return chicken to pan. Simmer 3 more mins until cooked through.
  5. 5Taste and season. Garnish with parsley and serve over rice.
💡 Highlight Tips
Slice chicken thin so it stays tender — thick pieces dry out fast.
Blend half the sweetcorn before adding for an even creamier sauce.
A pinch of chilli flakes at the end adds a nice kick without overpowering.
Italian · EP2
Chicken Mushroom Fusilli 🍝
Saucy, rich & endlessly satisfying
Cook time25 min
DifficultyEasy
Serves2
Ingredients
  • 200g fusilli pasta
  • 2 chicken thighs, diced
  • 150g mushrooms, sliced
  • 1 tin chopped tomatoes
  • 3 tbsp double cream
  • 1 tsp smoked paprika
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • Olive oil, salt, pepper, fresh coriander
Method
  1. 1Cook fusilli in salted water until al dente. Reserve a cup of pasta water.
  2. 2Fry chicken in olive oil until golden. Add mushrooms and cook for 3 mins.
  3. 3Add garlic and paprika. Stir 1 min, then add tomatoes. Simmer 8 mins.
  4. 4Stir in cream. Add cooked pasta and splash of pasta water to loosen.
  5. 5Toss well, season and top with fresh coriander.
💡 Highlight Tips
Don't skip the pasta water — the starch helps the sauce cling to every piece of fusilli.
Chicken thighs stay juicier than breast — worth the swap.
Smoked paprika is the secret depth here. Regular paprika won't hit the same.
Korean · EP2
Korean Fried Chicken 🍗
Sticky, crispy & dangerously good
Cook time40 min
DifficultyMedium
Serves2–3
Ingredients
  • 700g chicken wings
  • 4 tbsp gochujang paste
  • 2 tbsp honey or rice syrup
  • 1 tbsp soy sauce
  • 1 tbsp sesame oil
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • ½ cup potato starch (or cornflour)
  • Oil for deep frying
  • Sesame seeds & spring onion to finish
Method
  1. 1Pat wings dry. Toss in potato starch until fully coated. Shake off excess.
  2. 2First fry: 170°C for 8 mins. Remove and rest on a rack for 5 mins.
  3. 3Second fry: crank to 190°C, fry again for 4–5 mins until ultra crispy.
  4. 4Mix gochujang, honey, soy, sesame oil and garlic in a pan over low heat until glossy.
  5. 5Toss hot wings in sauce immediately. Top with sesame seeds and spring onion.
💡 Highlight Tips
Double frying is the key — first fry cooks it through, second fry makes it shatteringly crispy.
Potato starch over flour gives that signature light, crackling crust.
Toss in sauce right before serving — don't let them sit or they'll go soggy.
Asian · EP1
Asian Poached Chicken 🍜
Delicate, light & full of umami
Cook time30 min
DifficultyEasy
Serves1–2
Ingredients
  • 2 chicken breasts
  • 1L chicken stock
  • 2 slices ginger, 2 cloves garlic
  • 100g shimeji mushrooms
  • 80g glass noodles
  • Handful bok choy or greens
  • 2 tbsp chilli oil
  • 1 tbsp soy sauce, sesame oil, spring onion
Method
  1. 1Bring stock to a gentle simmer with ginger and garlic. Add chicken breasts.
  2. 2Poach on very low heat for 18–20 mins until just cooked through. Remove and slice.
  3. 3In the same broth, cook noodles, mushrooms and greens for 2–3 mins.
  4. 4Mix chilli oil, soy and a splash of broth for dipping sauce.
  5. 5Serve chicken over noodles in the broth with chilli sauce on the side.
💡 Highlight Tips
Never boil — a rolling boil toughens the chicken. Keep it at the gentlest simmer.
Rest the chicken in the broth off the heat for 5 mins for extra juiciness.
Chilli oil quality makes this dish — use a good one with crispy bits.
European · EP1
Salmon & Mushroom Orzo 🐟
Creamy, comforting & restaurant-worthy
Cook time35 min
DifficultyMedium
Serves2
Ingredients
  • 2 salmon fillets
  • 180g orzo pasta
  • 150g mushrooms, sliced
  • ½ red onion, diced
  • 2 tsp whole grain mustard
  • 100ml double cream
  • 300ml chicken or veg stock
  • Butter, garlic, lemon, fresh dill
Method
  1. 1Season salmon. Sear skin-side down in butter for 4 mins, flip for 2 mins. Set aside.
  2. 2In the same pan, fry red onion and mushrooms until caramelised, about 5 mins.
  3. 3Add garlic and orzo. Toast 1 min, then pour in stock. Stir and simmer 10–12 mins.
  4. 4Stir in cream, mustard and a squeeze of lemon. Season to taste.
  5. 5Place salmon on top. Finish with dill and a crack of black pepper.
💡 Highlight Tips
Start skin-side down and don't move it — you'll get a perfectly crispy skin.
Toast the orzo before adding stock for a nuttier, more complex flavour.
Whole grain mustard adds texture — don't substitute for smooth.
Italian · EP1
Gnocchi al Pomodoro 🍅
Bold, rustic & deeply satisfying
Cook time20 min
DifficultyEasy
Serves2
Ingredients
  • 500g fresh or vacuum-packed gnocchi
  • 1 tin San Marzano tomatoes
  • 3 cloves garlic, sliced thin
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • Handful fresh basil
  • Good quality olive oil
  • Salt, pepper, Parmesan to serve
Method
  1. 1Fry garlic in generous olive oil on low heat until just golden — don't burn it.
  2. 2Crush tomatoes into the pan by hand. Add sugar, season well. Simmer 12–15 mins.
  3. 3Cook gnocchi in boiling salted water until they float (2–3 mins). Drain.
  4. 4Toss gnocchi straight into the tomato sauce. Finish with torn basil.
  5. 5Plate up and hit with a good drizzle of olive oil and Parmesan.
💡 Highlight Tips
San Marzano tomatoes are sweeter — worth the extra £1 over regular tinned.
Don't overcook gnocchi — the moment they float, they're done.
A finishing drizzle of good olive oil transforms this from simple to special.
Italian · EP1
Black Garlic Linguine 🧄
Complex, umami-rich & cheffy
Cook time25 min
DifficultyMedium
Serves2
Ingredients
  • 200g De Cecco linguine
  • 1 whole head black garlic
  • 2 Italian sausages, skin removed
  • 100g tenderstem broccoli
  • 3 tbsp olive oil
  • Pinch chilli flakes
  • Parmesan, salt, black pepper
Method
  1. 1Cook linguine in well-salted water until al dente. Reserve pasta water.
  2. 2Squeeze black garlic cloves into a pan with olive oil. Mash into a paste over low heat.
  3. 3Add sausage meat in chunks. Fry until golden and caramelised, breaking up as you go.
  4. 4Blanch broccoli for 2 mins. Add to the pan with chilli flakes.
  5. 5Toss in drained linguine with a splash of pasta water. Finish with Parmesan.
💡 Highlight Tips
Black garlic tastes nothing like raw garlic — it's sweet, balsamic, deeply complex. Don't sub it.
Mash the black garlic into the oil early — it becomes an incredible sauce base.
Italian sausage > regular sausage here. The fennel and herbs make the dish.
